urduTranslation: اللہ کے نام کے ساتھ (کھانا شروع کرتا ہوں)۔
fadhail: The Prophet ﷺ commanded that whenever someone begins eating, they should say Bismillah. If one forgets at the start and remembers mid-meal, they should say 'Bismillahi fi awwalihi wa akhirih' — covering both the beginning and end of the meal.

englishTranslation: In the name of Allah at its beginning and at its end.
urduTranslation: اللہ کے نام کے ساتھ (کھانا شروع کرتا ہوں) اس کے شروع اور اس کے آخر میں۔
fadhail: This is recited when one forgets to say Bismillah at the start of the meal and remembers during eating. It covers the entirety of the meal from beginning to end, and shaytan is expelled from the food he had already partaken of.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَأَطْعِمْنَا خَيْرًا مِنْهُ
transliteration: Allahumma barik lana fihi wa at'imna khayran minh.
englishTranslation: O Allah! Bless it for us and feed us better than it.
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! ہمارے لیے اس میں برکت عطا کر اور ہمیں اس سے بہتر کھلا۔
fadhail: This dua is recited when Allah provides one with food (non-dairy). It asks for blessing in what has been given and for something even better in the future — combining gratitude with hopeful supplication.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ
transliteration: Allahumma barik lana fihi wa zidna minh.
englishTranslation: O Allah! Bless it for us and give us more of it.
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! ہمارے لیے اس میں برکت فرما اور ہمیں اس سے بھی زیادہ دے۔
fadhail: This dua is specifically recited when Allah provides milk or a dairy-based drink. Since milk is a complete food that combines both drink and nourishment, one asks for more of it rather than something better.

arabicText: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنِي هَذَا وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلَا قُوَّةٍ
transliteration: Alhamdulillahilladhi at'amani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah.
englishTranslation: All praise is for Allah who fed me this and provided it for me without any power or strength on my part.
urduTranslation: ہر قسم کی تعریف اللہ ہی کے لیے ہے جس نے مجھے یہ (کھانا) کھلایا اور مجھے یہ (کھانا) عطا کیا بغیر میری کسی طاقت کے اور بغیر کسی قوت کے۔
fadhail: This is the first dua after finishing eating. Whoever recites it after a meal will have his past sins forgiven. It attributes all provision entirely to Allah's grace, acknowledging that man has no independent power or strength.

arabicText: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ حَمْدًا كَثِيرًا طَيِّبًا مُبَارَكًا فِيهِ غَيْرَ مَكْفِيٍّ وَلَا مُوَدَّعٍ وَلَا مُسْتَغْنًى عَنْهُ رَبَّنَا
transliteration: Alhamdulillahi hamdan kathiran tayyiban mubarakan fih, ghayra makfiyyin wa la muwadda'in wa la mustaghnan 'anhu rabbana.
englishTranslation: All praise is for Allah — abundant, pure, and blessed praise. Praise that is not enough, nor bade farewell to, nor dispensed with, O our Lord.
urduTranslation: ہر قسم کی تعریف اللہ ہی کے لیے ہے بہت زیادہ، پاکیزہ اور اس میں برکت ڈالی گئی ہے، نہ (یہ کھانا) کافی کیا گیا (کہ مزید کی ضرورت نہ رہے) اور نہ اسے وداع کیا گیا اور نہ اس سے بے نیاز ہوا جاسکتا ہے، اے ہمارے رب۔
fadhail: This is the second dua after finishing eating. The Prophet ﷺ himself recited this after meals. It is a uniquely comprehensive praise — acknowledging that Allah's provision is never truly enough to repay His generosity, never to be abandoned, and one can never be independent of it.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَهُمْ فِيمَا رَزَقْتَهُمْ وَاغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَارْحَمْهُمْ
transliteration: Allahumma barik lahum fima razaqtahum, waghfir lahum warhamhum.
englishTranslation: O Allah! Bless them in what You have provided for them, forgive them, and have mercy on them.
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! ان کے لیے ان چیزوں میں برکت فرما جو تو نے ان کو دیں اور انہیں معاف فرما اور ان پر رحم فرما۔
fadhail: This dua is recited by the guest for the host after eating. It is a beautiful expression of gratitude toward the host, asking Allah to bless their provision, forgive them, and shower them with mercy.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ أَطْعِمْ مَنْ أَطْعَمَنِي وَاسْقِ مَنْ سَقَانِي
transliteration: Allahumma at'im man at'amani wasqi man saqani.
englishTranslation: O Allah! Feed the one who fed me and give drink to the one who gave me drink.
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! اسے کھلا جس نے مجھے کھلایا اور اسے پلا جس نے مجھے پلایا۔
fadhail: This is recited by the person who has been given food or drink when the host does not eat with them. It is a heartfelt dua asking Allah to repay the host with His own provision.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي ثَمَرِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مَدِينَتِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي صَاعِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مُدِّنَا
transliteration: Allahumma barik lana fi thamarana wa barik lana fi madinатina wa barik lana fi sa'ina wa barik lana fi muddina.
englishTranslation: O Allah! Bless us in our fruits and bless us in our city and bless us in our Sa' (measure) and bless us in our Mudd (measure).
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! ہمارے لیے ہمارے پھل میں برکت فرما اور ہمارے لیے ہمارے شہر میں برکت فرما اور ہمارے لیے ہمارے صاع (ماپنے کے پیمانے) میں برکت فرما اور ہمارے لیے ہمارے مد میں برکت فرما۔
fadhail: This dua is recited when one sees a new fruit for the first time in the season. It asks for comprehensive blessing — in fruits, city, and measures of provision — making it a dua for abundance and barakah in all sustenance.

arabicText: اَللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ كَسَوْتَنِيهِ أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِهِ وَخَيْرِ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهِ وَشَرِّ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ
transliteration: Allahumma lakal-hamdu anta kasawtanihi, as'aluka min khayrihi wa khayri ma suni'a lah, wa a'udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni'a lah.
englishTranslation: O Allah! All praise is Yours. You have clothed me with it. I ask You for its good and the good of what it was made for, and I seek refuge in You from its evil and the evil of what it was made for.
urduTranslation: اے اللہ! تیرے ہی لیے تعریف ہے، تجھی نے مجھے یہ پہنایا، میں تجھ سے اس کی بھلائی اور اس کام کی بھلائی کا جس کے لیے اسے بنایا گیا ہے سوال کرتا ہوں اور میں اس کے شر سے اور اس کام کے شر سے جس کے لیے اسے بنایا گیا ہے تیری پناہ میں آتا ہوں۔
fadhail: This dua is recited when wearing new clothes. It praises Allah for the blessing of clothing, seeks the good purpose the garment was made for, and seeks protection from any harm associated with it.

englishTranslation: May it wear out and may Allah replace it.
urduTranslation: تم اسے بوسیدہ کرو اور اللہ تعالیٰ (تمہیں) اس کے عوض اور دے۔
fadhail: This is recited by another person for the one wearing new clothes — a dua wishing that the garment is worn until it wears out and that Allah replaces it with another. It is a blessed supplication from one Muslim to another upon receiving new clothing.

arabicText: اِلْبَسْ جَدِيدًا وَعِشْ حَمِيدًا وَمُتْ شَهِيدًا
transliteration: Ilbas jadidan wa 'ish hamidan wa mut shahida.
englishTranslation: Wear new clothes, live a praiseworthy life, and die as a martyr.
urduTranslation: نیا لباس پہنو، قابلِ تعریف زندگی گزارو اور شہادت کی موت مرو۔
fadhail: This is the second dua recited by others for the one wearing new clothes. It encompasses a beautiful wish for the person: the enjoyment of new clothing, a life lived with honor and praise, and the highest death — martyrdom in the way of Allah.
